2605.04965.title: Reliable Modeling of Distribution Shifts via Displacement-Reshaped Optimal Transport
2605.04965.paper_summary: Optimal transport (OT) is a central framework for modeling distribution shifts. Because OT compares distributions directly in input space, a well-designed ground metric between observations is essential to ensure that the optimizer does not violate the true geometry of change. We propose Displacement-Reshaped Optimal Transport (ReshapeOT), a method that reshapes the ground metric by integrating observed sample displacements as an additional source of knowledge. Technically, ReshapeOT replaces the Euclidean metric with a Mahalanobis distance estimated from displacement second moments. This effectively carves expressways through the input space, inviting transport solutions that better align with observed displacements. Our method is computationally lightweight, integrates seamlessly into any OT solver that operates on a cost matrix, and can be kernelized for further flexibility. Experiments on synthetic and real-world data show that ReshapeOT achieves substantial gains in transport reliability. We further demonstrate our method's usefulness in two practical use cases.
2605.04965.paper_summary_zh: 最佳運輸（OT）是建模分佈變化的核心框架。由於 OT 直接在輸入空間中比較分佈，因此在觀察之間設計良好的基準度量是至關重要的，以確保優化器不會違反變化的真實幾何。 我們提出了位移重塑最佳運輸（ReshapeOT），這是一種通過將觀察到的樣本位移整合為額外的知識來源來重塑基準度量的方法。 從技術上講，ReshapeOT 用從位移二次矩估計的馬哈拉諾比斯距離替代歐幾里得度量。 這有效地在輸入空間中開鑿了快速通道，邀請更好地與觀察到的位移對齊的運輸解決方案。 我們的方法計算輕量級，無縫集成到任何在成本矩陣上運行的 OT 求解器中，並且可以進行核化以獲得進一步的靈活性。 在合成數據和現實世界數據上的實驗顯示，ReshapeOT 在運輸可靠性方面取得了顯著的增益。 我們進一步展示了我們方法在兩個實際用例中的有效性。
2605.04965.author: Philip Naumann et.al.
2605.04965.authors: Philip Naumann, Jacob Kauffmann, Klaus-Robert Müller, Grégoire Montavon
